method_atom: a second-quantized field-theoretic framework in which a large number of identical discrete fundamental quanta are the microscopic degrees of freedom, and a macroscopic continuum phase is argued to emerge as a collective many-body condensate (a Bose-Einstein-type ground state) reached through a phase transition, with the effective large-scale dynamics extracted from the underlying quanta's field theory.
object_atom: continuum spacetime and geometry in non-perturbative quantum gravity, treated as arising from discrete quantum structures of loop and simplicial quantum gravity via the group field theory formalism.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the condensed-matter/field-theory machinery of a continuum emerging as a condensate of many discrete quanta; the domain-specific object is quantum spacetime/geometry, which is somewhat fused with the method since the group field theory is defined specifically for the gravitational quanta.
